tracks.title: Adaptive Anomaly Detection with Continual Learning
tracks.summary: On-device adaptive scoring that learns local baselines rather than relying on fixed thresholds or periodic cloud retraining.
tracks.idea: TinyML-style continual learning with replay buffer, Laplace noise for differential privacy, and Halo2 circuits for update integrity proofs.
tracks.matters: Edge devices drift over time. A static detector becomes unreliable within weeks of deployment in most real environments.
tracks.state: Adaptive multi-signal EWMA scoring is implemented with a bounded replay buffer providing windowed statistics. Adaptation controls (freeze, decay, reset) support safe baseline management. Missing: continual learning loop, differential privacy, and proof-carrying updates.

tracks.title: Formal Verification of Detection Rules
tracks.summary: Represent detection logic as a formally specified state machine and verify runtime conformance against that specification.
tracks.idea: Embedded TLA+-to-Rust model checker with a zk-SNARK for rule-satisfaction proofs.
tracks.matters: Shifts the correctness argument from 'it seems to work' to 'we can state and check what correctness means.'
tracks.state: A PolicyStateMachine records and validates all threat-level transitions against formally defined legal rules. The state machine can be exported as TLA+ and Alloy modules for offline verification. Missing: automated model-checking backend.

tracks.title: Cross-Device Swarm Intelligence
tracks.summary: Privacy-preserving collaborative detection where devices share partial threat signals without exposing raw telemetry.
tracks.idea: Private set intersection combined with federated averaging and ZK proofs of honest participation.
tracks.matters: Many real attacks only become visible when low-confidence evidence is aggregated across a fleet.
tracks.state: No cross-device communication exists in the prototype.

tracks.title: Quantum-Walk Propagation Modeling
tracks.summary: Use classical simulation of quantum walks to predict how anomalies spread through mesh topologies.
tracks.idea: Discrete-time quantum walk simulation on device topology graphs with learned damping factors.
tracks.matters: Would shift SentinelEdge from purely reactive detection toward predictive isolation planning.
tracks.state: No propagation graph or predictive spread model exists yet.

tracks.title: Model Poisoning Detection and Recovery
tracks.summary: Detect when the local model or policy has been tampered with, then recover to a verified safe state.
tracks.idea: Spectral poisoning analysis combined with Merkle-rooted model checkpoints and cryptographic recovery proofs.
tracks.matters: A detector that can be poisoned without noticing is a fundamentally weak security primitive.
tracks.state: Four poisoning heuristics (mean shift, variance spike, drift accumulation, auth burst) analyze replay buffers for manipulation attempts. Adaptation controls allow freezing baselines during suspected poisoning. Missing: verified checkpoint rollback and recovery proofs.

tracks.title: Energy-Aware Verifiable Isolation
tracks.summary: Select mitigations that respect both security urgency and the device energy budget, with proofs that the action matched policy.
tracks.idea: Priority queue with energy cost model plus a proof circuit for compliance verification.
tracks.matters: Edge security cannot assume desktop-class power or cooling. The mitigation has to fit the device.
tracks.state: Energy-aware downgrade logic and pluggable action adapters (logging, throttle, quarantine, isolate) are implemented. Missing: formal proof mechanisms.

tracks.title: Self-Healing Network Reconfiguration
tracks.summary: After isolating compromised nodes, automatically repair the network topology while preserving security invariants.
tracks.idea: Graph repair algorithms with zk-SNARK proofs that the new configuration maintains stated invariants.
tracks.matters: Isolation without recovery turns defensive action into self-inflicted outage.
tracks.state: No topology model or repair engine exists yet.

tracks.title: Privacy-Preserving Coordinated Response
tracks.summary: Let devices coordinate defensive actions (e.g., collective quarantine) without revealing individual sensor data.
tracks.idea: Secure multi-party computation adapted for low-power device constraints.
tracks.matters: Fleet-level response becomes practical when it does not require a centralised view of everything.
tracks.state: No secure multi-party coordination path exists yet.

tracks.title: Adaptive Response Strength
tracks.summary: Map detection confidence and local constraints into different response intensities rather than a single fixed action.
tracks.idea: Continuous response scaling: observe → rate-limit → quarantine → rollback-and-escalate, shaped by a learned policy with energy penalty.
tracks.matters: Prevents overreaction on benign spikes and underreaction on genuinely dangerous events.
tracks.state: Threat score and battery state shape response selection. Pluggable adapters dispatch concrete actions through a composable chain. Adaptation mode controls (Normal, Frozen, Decay) further refine detector sensitivity.

tracks.title: Verifiable Rollback and Forensic Recovery
tracks.summary: Restore device state to a known-safe checkpoint and preserve a verifiable record of what was changed.
tracks.idea: Merkle-based snapshotting with ZK range proofs for restoration integrity.
tracks.matters: Recovery is far more credible when it can be replayed and audited after the incident.
tracks.state: Rollback checkpoints capture detector and device state on severe/critical events. Adapter-backed restore reapplies isolation/quarantine state. Forensic bundle exporter produces human-readable evidence reports. Proof-carrying updates bind every baseline change with SHA-256 cryptographic evidence. Witness export bundles are available for future ZK integration. Missing: Merkle proofs and actual ZK verification.

tracks.title: Post-Quantum Secure Audit Logs
tracks.summary: Tamper-evident event logs signed with algorithms that remain viable in a post-quantum setting.
tracks.idea: Hybrid classical + PQ signature scheme (Dilithium/Falcon) with a seamless in-place upgrade path.
tracks.matters: The entire 'verifiable security' claim depends on the evidence trail remaining trustworthy long-term.
tracks.state: SHA-256 cryptographic digest chain and signed audit checkpoints are implemented. End-to-end chain verification is operational. Missing: post-quantum signature upgrade.

tracks.title: Zero-Knowledge Device State Proof
tracks.summary: Prove that a device was in a particular historical state without disclosing the underlying data.
tracks.idea: Recursive zk-SNARKs over Merkle history for privacy-preserving attestation.
tracks.matters: Allows audits and incident response without exposing full device contents to the auditor.
tracks.state: Proof backend interface with witness export exists (DigestBackend and ZkStubBackend). Missing: actual ZK circuit implementation.

tracks.title: Regulatory-Compliant Selective Disclosure
tracks.summary: Export only the subset of logs a regulator requires while proving the rest was not altered.
tracks.idea: zk-SNARK-based log redaction with integrity proofs.
tracks.matters: Many deployments need to satisfy auditability and privacy constraints simultaneously.
tracks.state: Forensic bundle exports provide structured evidence subsets. FP/FN benchmark harness enables precision/recall/F1 measurement for regulatory compliance evidence. ZK-based redaction proofs remain deferred.

tracks.title: Energy-Harvesting Archival Scheduling
tracks.summary: Defer expensive archival operations until harvested energy (solar, scavenged) is available.
tracks.idea: Predictive harvesting model combined with deferred compaction scheduling.
tracks.matters: Long-lived remote edge devices often operate under severe, variable energy constraints.
tracks.state: No archival scheduler exists yet.

tracks.title: Cross-Device Threat Intelligence Sharing
tracks.summary: Share anonymised threat indicators across devices with proof of provenance and integrity.
tracks.idea: Privacy-preserving set union with signed, verifiable provenance chains.
tracks.matters: Shared threat signatures become more valuable when receivers can verify their origin.
tracks.state: No threat-intelligence exchange protocol exists yet.

tracks.title: Hardware Root-of-Trust Integration
tracks.summary: Bind critical keys or trust anchors to TPM / secure enclave hardware where available.
tracks.idea: Conditional compilation with hardware-attested key storage and a software fallback path.
tracks.matters: The runtime becomes substantially harder to subvert when root secrets are not just files on disk.
tracks.state: No hardware-attestation path exists yet.

tracks.title: Wasm-Based Extensible Policies
tracks.summary: Let operators ship custom detection or response logic as sandboxed WebAssembly modules.
tracks.idea: Sandboxed Wasm interpreter with resource accounting and energy compliance verification.
tracks.matters: Opens the project to domain-specific extension without forking the core runtime.
tracks.state: No Wasm policy surface or sandbox exists yet.

tracks.title: Energy-Proportional Model Quantization
tracks.summary: Adjust model precision dynamically to save energy while proving accuracy stayed above a threshold.
tracks.idea: Quantization-aware training combined with ZK accuracy proofs.
tracks.matters: Edge deployments routinely trade precision for power. The question is whether you can do that without losing trust in the result.
tracks.state: The prototype does not include quantized models.

tracks.title: Causal False-Positive Reduction
tracks.summary: Use lightweight causal inference to distinguish real threats from noisy correlations.
tracks.idea: Tiny on-device causal graph inference for anomaly signal filtering.
tracks.matters: False positives are one of the fastest ways to erode operator trust in any detection system.
tracks.state: No causal inference layer exists yet.

tracks.title: Supply-Chain Firmware Attestation
tracks.summary: Prove that the running firmware and model artifacts match a known-good vendor-signed build.
tracks.idea: Remote attestation combined with Merkle root verification of the binary.
tracks.matters: Strengthens trust at the base layer — before runtime detection even begins.
tracks.state: No firmware or model attestation path exists yet.

tracks.title: Quantum-Resistant Key Rotation
tracks.summary: Periodic key rotation using post-quantum algorithms, optimised for battery life.
tracks.idea: Key ratcheting combined with energy-aware scheduling of expensive PQ operations.
tracks.matters: Key hygiene matters, but heavy cryptography can be prohibitively expensive on small devices.
tracks.state: The prototype has no key lifecycle subsystem.

tracks.title: Cross-Platform Binary Self-Optimisation
tracks.summary: Let the runtime specialise itself for different target architectures and energy profiles.
tracks.idea: JIT-like architecture-aware specialisation for heterogeneous edge hardware.
tracks.matters: The project targets edge devices, so hardware diversity is part of the problem space.
tracks.state: No architecture-specific specialisation logic exists yet.

tracks.title: Verifiable Swarm Defence Coordination
tracks.summary: Multiple devices vote on threats with ZK-verifiable tallying, so no single node must be blindly trusted.
tracks.idea: Threshold signatures combined with privacy-preserving voting and Byzantine-fault-tolerant coordination.
tracks.matters: Collective defence becomes practical when individual participants cannot be forced to lie undetectably.
tracks.state: No multi-device voting or swarm defence layer exists yet.

tracks.title: Energy-Harvesting Posture Adjustment
tracks.summary: Adapt cryptographic or defensive intensity based on predicted near-term energy availability.
tracks.idea: Predictive energy-aware security posture scheduling with formal trade-off guarantees.
tracks.matters: A node on scarce harvested power needs a different security posture than one with mains power.
tracks.state: No energy forecasting or posture scheduler exists yet.

tracks.title: Evolutionary Model Improvement
tracks.summary: On-device bounded evolutionary search for long-term detection model improvement without cloud dependency.
tracks.idea: Genetic algorithms with ZK fitness proofs, operating on a months-long timescale.
tracks.matters: The longest-horizon path toward self-improving edge detection without permanent cloud reliance.
tracks.state: No long-horizon model adaptation system exists yet.

tracks.title: Explainable Anomaly Attribution
tracks.summary: On-device interpretable attribution tracing each anomaly score to contributing signals and temporal context.
tracks.idea: Shapley-value approximation adapted for streaming edge data with bounded compute budget.
tracks.matters: Operators need to understand why an alert fired before they can trust and act on it.
tracks.state: Per-signal contribution breakdown is included in every anomaly report. Human-readable reasons explain each anomalous axis. Missing: formal Shapley-value attribution.

tracks.title: Federated Threat Model Distillation
tracks.summary: Fleet-wide model improvement through federated learning rounds distilled into compact student models.
tracks.idea: Federated averaging with knowledge distillation, differential privacy, and proof-of-participation.
tracks.matters: Individual devices see limited threat diversity; federated distillation lets the fleet learn collectively.
tracks.state: No federated learning or model distillation infrastructure exists yet.

tracks.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing
tracks.summary: Automated red-team harness generating adversarial telemetry sequences to probe detector weaknesses.
tracks.idea: Gradient-free black-box attack synthesis (genetic perturbation, boundary probing) with coverage metrics.
tracks.matters: A detector never probed adversarially is likely brittle against adaptive attackers.
tracks.state: Grammar-based adversarial test harness with three evasion strategies (SlowDrip, BurstMask, DriftInject) and decision-surface coverage metrics. Accessible via CLI harness command.

tracks.title: Temporal Logic Runtime Monitoring
tracks.summary: Lightweight monitor checking live telemetry against LTL/CTL safety and liveness properties.
tracks.idea: On-the-fly LTL monitoring with bounded history, automaton compilation, and violation attestation.
tracks.matters: Explicit temporal properties enable guarantees like 'a critical alert is always followed by a response within N samples.'
tracks.state: Temporal-logic runtime monitor with safety and bounded-liveness property checking is integrated into the execution pipeline. State machine transitions are monitored for legal transition compliance.

tracks.title: Anomaly Correlation Graph Mining
tracks.summary: Construct lightweight causal correlation graphs across signal dimensions for multi-stage attack detection.
tracks.idea: Incremental Granger-causality estimation on windowed replay buffers with graph-based alert clustering.
tracks.matters: Many advanced attacks present as individually benign signals that become suspicious only when correlated.
tracks.state: Pearson-based multi-signal correlation engine is integrated into the runtime pipeline. Server-side /api/correlation endpoint exposes live analysis. Co-rising signal detection identifies coordinated anomalies.

tracks.title: Digital Twin Fleet Simulation
tracks.summary: Deterministic simulation harness modelling heterogeneous edge fleets for scenario testing.
tracks.idea: Discrete-event simulation with pluggable device profiles, attack injection, and differential comparison.
tracks.matters: Testing on a simulated fleet is cheaper and safer than experimenting on production hardware.
tracks.state: Deterministic test fixtures exist for single-device scenarios. No fleet-scale simulation.

tracks.title: Autonomous Secure Patch Management
tracks.summary: Self-patching runtime that verifies patch integrity and proves post-patch state correctness.
tracks.idea: Signed delta patches with Merkle-rooted binary verification and automatic rollback on failure.
tracks.matters: Manual patching at scale is infeasible; automated patching without verification is a supply-chain risk.
tracks.state: No patch management or binary update mechanism exists.

tracks.title: Deception-Based Threat Engagement
tracks.summary: Lightweight honeypots and canary tokens at the edge to detect lateral movement and reconnaissance.
tracks.idea: Synthetic service emulation with canary credential injection and automated alert escalation.
tracks.matters: Deception forces attackers to reveal themselves by interacting with assets legitimate users never touch.
tracks.state: No deception layer or canary token system exists.

tracks.title: Secure Multi-Tenancy Isolation
tracks.summary: Namespace-isolated detection and response policies on shared edge hardware.
tracks.idea: Per-tenant policy scoping, resource accounting, and cryptographic namespace separation.
tracks.matters: Shared edge gateways often serve multiple tenants who must not see each other's data.
tracks.state: The runtime is single-tenant. No namespace isolation exists.

tracks.title: Side-Channel Attack Detection
tracks.summary: Detect timing, power, and EM side-channel attacks via statistical profiling of device operations.
tracks.idea: High-frequency sampling of execution timing and power draw with anomaly detection on side-channel signals.
tracks.matters: Side-channel attacks bypass software defenses entirely; detecting them requires hardware-layer observation.
tracks.state: All detection is software-telemetry based. No hardware-level side-channel profiling.

tracks.title: Edge-Cloud Hybrid Offload
tracks.summary: Decide when to offload expensive analysis to the cloud with verifiable computation receipts.
tracks.idea: Selective offload policy with encrypted transfer, verifiable computation receipts, and local validation.
tracks.matters: Some analyses are too expensive for edge hardware, but blind cloud offload sacrifices privacy and autonomy.
tracks.state: Runtime is fully local. No cloud offload path exists.

tracks.title: Resilient Mesh Self-Organisation
tracks.summary: Autonomous mesh network formation and repair maintaining security invariants after node loss.
tracks.idea: Distributed spanning-tree repair with cryptographic neighbour authentication and convergence proofs.
tracks.matters: Edge networks lose nodes to failure and compromise; automatic repair is a prerequisite for collective defense.
tracks.state: No topology awareness or mesh networking exists.

tracks.title: Behavioural Device Fingerprinting
tracks.summary: Behavioural identity based on operational patterns for impersonation detection without shared secrets.
tracks.idea: Statistical device profiling from telemetry baselines with drift-based identity verification.
tracks.matters: Device impersonation is a common edge attack; behavioural fingerprints provide a hard-to-forge second factor.
tracks.state: Behavioural device fingerprinting module with statistical profiling across all telemetry dimensions and z-score-based impersonation detection is implemented.

tracks.title: Formal Policy Composition
tracks.summary: Compose multiple policies with formal guarantees against contradictions and deadlocks.
tracks.idea: Policy algebra with automated conflict detection, priority resolution, and model-checked composition proofs.
tracks.matters: Realistic deployments layer vendor, operator, and regulatory policies that can silently conflict.
tracks.state: A single policy engine maps scores to actions. No multi-policy composition exists.

tracks.title: Privacy-Preserving Incident Forensics
tracks.summary: Cross-device forensic analysis without exposing raw sensor data to investigators.
tracks.idea: Secure multi-party computation for forensic queries with ZK evidence-consistency proofs.
tracks.matters: Incident response teams need evidence, but sharing raw telemetry can violate privacy regulations.
tracks.state: Single-device forensic export exists. No multi-device privacy-preserving forensic protocol.
